architecture.summary: A neuro-symbolic multi-agent system that constructs and aggregates knowledge graphs across spatially proximate agents to facilitate cooperation. Individual agents learn rich heterogeneous representations via a graph autoencoder (GAE). When agents enter a bridging zone, their knowledge graphs are aggregated into hypergraphs using density-adaptive k-NN construction with Gabriel pruning for proximity graph building. The aggregated structures are then used to improve policy learning in a communication-oriented environment.

biblio.title: Bridging Heterogeneous Agents: A Neuro-Symbolic Knowledge Transfer Approach

claims.statement: improvements of approximately 10% in episode rewards compared to Deep Q-Network (DQN) baselines
up to 40% in individual agent rewards compared to Deep Q-Network (DQN) baselines
maintaining comparable policy loss values with DQN baselines
derive the theoretical bounds on the minimum number of hyperedges required for connectivity under arity and locality constraints
density-adaptive k-nearest neighbor (k-NN) construction with Gabriel pruning balances local density awareness with geometric consistency in proximity graph building
the explicit graph structures may offer interpretability benefits for applications requiring auditability
